2. Social Proof and Validation: Human beings typically seek validation from their peers. When the validation you receive is negative, it might conflict with your internal assessment of your success, creating a cognitive dissonance where your self-worth is challenged.

3. Cognitive Biases: You might be experiencing confirmation bias, where you give more weight to negative feedback as it reinforces any underlying insecurities, no matter how small they may have been. This can lead to a loop where every critical comment is seen as a sign that you’re not measuring up, even if objectively that isn’t the case.

4. The Digital Context: The nature of online interactions can strip away the nuance of face-to-face communication. This can make criticism seem more harsh and personal, further destabilizing your confidence.
